title: Anthropic, Amazon, Microsoft, and Google publish a shared jailbreak severity scale
subtitle: The Cyber Jailbreak Severity framework grades AI jailbreaks CJS-0 through CJS-4 on four axes — the industry's first attempt at a common vocabulary for how dangerous a given exploit actually is.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: On July 2, 2026, Anthropic published the Cyber Jailbreak Severity (CJS) framework, developed jointly with Amazon, Microsoft, and Google. CJS is a shared five-level scale — CJS-0 (Informational) through CJS-4 (Critical) — for scoring how dangerous a demonstrated AI jailbreak actually is. Ratings are computed across four axes: capability gain (how far a technique takes an attacker beyond tools they already have), breadth of capability gain (how many distinct offensive tasks it works on), ease of weaponization, and discoverability. The bands are exponential, so each step up represents several times more real-world risk than the level below.

The framework is modeled directly on the Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) that software-security teams have used since 2005 to triage vulnerabilities. Its point is not novel science — it is shared vocabulary. Today the four labs disagree not only on whether a given jailbreak matters, but on what 'matters' means. A shared scale lets red-teamers, disclosure programs, and downstream users triage findings the same way across models, and gives regulators a language they can adopt without inventing one from scratch.

The launch was triggered by real damage. On June 12, the U.S. Commerce Department pulled Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 offline worldwide after Amazon researchers demonstrated a jailbreak that got Fable 5 to flag vulnerabilities in software repositories and, in one case, write demonstration exploit code. The models returned on July 1 with a new classifier that blocks that specific technique in more than 99% of attempts. Anthropic has not yet said how partner labs will resolve disagreements when they score the same jailbreak differently, or who arbitrates a dispute — CJS is explicitly a first draft.

Takeaway for learners: the practical field of AI safety is converging on the shape of classical information security — scored disclosures, coordinated release, cross-vendor severity rubrics — because that field already solved most of the coordination problem twenty years ago. If you are studying AI safety, read the CVSS 4.0 specification alongside the CJS draft. The mental model transfers, and the field is short on people who fluently speak both languages.

sources.title: AI Model Safety Standards Deal Targets August 1: Five Labs Adopt First Jailbreak Scoring Scale — TechTimes

Anthropic Redeploys Fable 5 With Cross-Lab Jailbreak Rubric — AI Weekly
